title: Bounded Prefetch, Worker Ownership, Ordering, and Backpressure · Runtime Contract: Pokedex Go Prefetch Value
summary: Every map result launches a goroutine, concurrency is unbounded, jobs block after cancellation, output follows completion order, and prefetched failures poison visible state. Predict the user decision, Go ownership, command state, API contract, resource budget, failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pokedex go prefetch value increment in bounded cancellable Pokemon prefetch pipeline. Learners progress through prediction, explic…
objectives: Define which next-view latency benefit justifies prefetch traffic and when no prefetch should occur.
Test cancel before, during, and after work; second interrupt policy; state preservation; output; goroutine completion; and idempotent cleanup.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Predict Pokedex Go Prefetch Value · Theory Pokedex Go Prefetch V…
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 6283 during a race-instrumented cache stress case. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce prefetch concurrency, queue, ordering, cancellation, and partial-failure ownership; no user-visible meaning may rely on color or cursor motion; then inject one redirect, control sequence, file, timeout, or persistence failure and reject it safely. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
